name: material-component-dev
description: FlowGram 物料组件开发指南 - 用于在 form-materials 包中创建新的物料组件
capabilities: Guide creation of React components in FlowGram form-materials package structure
Generate TypeScript component code with proper type definitions and exports
Create Storybook stories for component testing and documentation
Provide code templates following FlowGram naming and structure conventions
Suggest proper usage of Semi UI components and internal dependencies
Generate component prop interfaces with appropriate TypeScript types
author: bytedance
securitySummary: This skill is a pure documentation guide for React component development. All static findings are false positives from markdown documentation containing example commands, local URLs, and relative import paths. No executable code or security risks present.
valueStatement: Creating React components for FlowGram requires following specific patterns and conventions. This skill guides Claude Code through the complete component development workflow with TypeScript, Semi UI, and Storybook testing.
userTitle: Build FlowGram React Components with Best Practices
limitations: Only applicable to FlowGram project structure and conventions
Does not run automated tests or type checking directly
Requires manual verification in Storybook development environment
Limited to component patterns used in form-materials package

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers working on FlowGram component library
useCases.title: Create New Form Material Component
useCases.description: Build a new reusable form component for FlowGram with proper TypeScript types, Semi UI integration, and Storybook documentation following project conventions.

useCases.title: Add Component with Code Editor Integration
useCases.description: Develop components that integrate JSON or code editors from FlowGram internal packages with proper type safety and error handling.

useCases.title: Generate Storybook Documentation
useCases.description: Create comprehensive Storybook stories that demonstrate component usage with interactive examples and proper metadata configuration.

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic Component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new component called DataPicker in the form-materials package that allows users to select data fields from a schema.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new component from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Add Storybook Story
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Storybook story for the SchemaValidator component with examples showing validation success and error states.

promptTemplates.title: Integrate Code Editor
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a QueryBuilder component that uses JsonCodeEditor from internal packages and validates JSON Schema v7 format.

promptTemplates.title: Debug Type Errors
promptTemplates.prompt: Fix the TypeScript errors in FormBuilder component. The error says IJsonSchema type is not found and React refers to UMD global.

bestPractices: Always use named exports instead of default exports to enable tree shaking and improve bundle optimization.
Import React explicitly in all component files to avoid UMD global reference errors in TypeScript.
Keep component props minimal and focused on core functionality rather than adding excessive configuration options.
antiPatterns: Do not create separate npm packages for individual components. Keep all components in the form-materials package.
Avoid using external json-schema types. Always import IJsonSchema from internal packages.
Do not write unit tests for components. Use Storybook for manual testing and verification instead.

faq.question: Why use named exports instead of default exports?
faq.answer: Named exports enable better tree shaking in bundlers, making it easier to eliminate unused code and reduce bundle size. This is a project-wide convention for all FlowGram components.

faq.question: Which UI component library should I use?
faq.answer: Always use Semi UI components from the @douyinfe/semi-ui package. This ensures visual consistency across the FlowGram application and provides a comprehensive set of pre-built components.

faq.question: How do I test my component after creating it?
faq.answer: Run rush build:watch in one terminal to compile the package, then start Storybook with yarn dev in apps/demo-materials. Access your component story at localhost:6006 to test interactively.

faq.question: What is the correct way to import JSON Schema types?
faq.answer: Use IJsonSchema type from @flowgram.ai/json-schema package. Do not use external json-schema or JSONSchema7 types from other packages as they are not compatible with the project structure.

faq.question: Where should I place helper functions for my component?
faq.answer: Create a utils subdirectory within your component folder and place helper functions there with proper TypeScript types and copyright headers. Export functions using named exports.

faq.question: How do I fix React UMD global reference errors?
faq.answer: Add an explicit import React statement at the top of your file. This error occurs when React is used as a global in UMD builds but your file is an ES module.
